title: Move min-height CSS from Card Title to Card Header
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want card headers to maintain consistent minimum heights, so that the dashboard layout looks uniform regardless of title length.
description:
The `min-height: 55px` CSS property was incorrectly applied to `.card-title` instead of `.card .card-header`. This caused inconsistent card sizing because the min-height should apply to the entire header container, not just the title text element.

### Change
- Remove `min-height: 55px` from `.card-title`
- Apply `min-height: 55px` to `.card .card-header`
acceptance_criteria: The `.card-title` class no longer has a `min-height` property
Card headers render with consistent heights across the dashboard
ux_changes: Moved `min-height: 55px` from `.card-title` to `.card .card-header` for more consistent card header sizing across the dashboard.
implementation_notes: Simple CSS selector change. The min-height property was moved from the child `.card-title` element to the parent `.card .card-header` container.
comment_summary: Developer completed the change promptly. No issues reported.
